items.subject: mathematics
items.description: Area of parallelogram spanned by a, b.

items.subject: mathematics
items.latex: \det\begin{pmatrix} a & b \\ c & d \end{pmatrix} = ad - bc

items.subject: mathematics
items.description: Probability of k successes in n Bernoulli trials.

items.subject: physics
items.description: Restoring force of an ideal spring.

items.title: Gauss's Law (E)
items.subject: physics
items.description: Divergence of electric field from charge density.

items.title: Ampère–Maxwell
items.subject: physics
items.description: Magnetic field from currents and changing E.

items.title: Exponential Growth
items.subject: biology
items.description: Unlimited population growth at rate r.

items.title: Unit Circle
items.subject: mathematics
items.description: Point on the unit circle at angle θ.

items.title: Right Triangle
items.subject: mathematics
items.description: Right triangle with legs a, b and hypotenuse c.

items.title: Production Possibility Frontier
items.subject: economics
items.description: PPF: efficient vs inefficient production points.

items.title: Free-Body Sketch
items.subject: physics
items.description: Block with normal, weight, and applied force.

items.title: Sine Wave
items.subject: physics
items.description: Wave with amplitude A and wavelength λ.

items.title: Derivative (definition)
items.subject: mathematics
items.description: Limit definition motivates the tangent-line interpretation.
items.body: The derivative f′(a) is the instantaneous rate of change of f at a — the slope of the tangent line to y = f(x) at x = a.

items.title: Net Present Value
items.subject: finance
items.body: Present value of all cash inflows minus the present value of all cash outflows, discounted at the project’s cost of capital.

items.title: Limit laws checklist
items.subject: mathematics
listItems: Sum / difference: lim (f ± g) = lim f ± lim g
Quotient: lim (f/g) = (lim f)/(lim g) if lim g ≠ 0
Constant multiple: lim (c f) = c lim f

items.title: Check the domain
items.subject: mathematics
items.body: Before simplifying, note where expressions are undefined (division by zero, even roots of negatives, log of non-positive).

items.title: Unit consistency
items.subject: physics
items.body: Always convert to SI (or a consistent set) before substituting into formulas. Mixed units are a common source of wrong answers.

items.title: Newton iteration (pseudocode)
items.subject: mathematics
items.code:
x ← x0
for k = 1..N:
  if |f(x)| < tol: return x
  x ← x − f(x) / f'(x)
return x

items.title: NPV accumulation
items.subject: finance
items.code:
npv = 0
for t, cf in enumerate(cashflows):
  npv += cf / (1 + r)**t
return npv

items.title: Speed of light
items.subject: physics
items.body: Exact defined value in SI is 299 792 458 m/s.

items.title: Supply & demand
items.subject: economics
items.description: Linear supply and demand with equilibrium.

items.title: Limit (definition)
items.subject: mathematics
items.body: We write limₓ→a f(x) = L when f(x) approaches L as x approaches a (from both sides unless one-sided). Formal ε–δ: for every ε > 0 there exists δ > 0 such that 0 < |x−a| < δ implies |f(x)−L| < ε.

items.title: Definite integral
items.subject: mathematics
items.body: ∫ₐᵇ f(x) dx is the net signed area under y = f(x) from x = a to x = b. By FTC, if F′ = f then ∫ₐᵇ f = F(b) − F(a).

items.title: Eigenvalue
items.subject: mathematics
items.body: A scalar λ is an eigenvalue of square matrix A if there exists nonzero v with Av = λv. The vector v is an eigenvector for λ.

items.title: Continuity at a point
items.subject: mathematics
items.body: f is continuous at a if limₓ→a f(x) exists, f(a) is defined, and limₓ→a f(x) = f(a).

items.title: Variance
items.subject: mathematics
items.body: Var(X) = E[(X − μ)²] measures spread of a random variable about its mean μ = E[X]. Also Var(X) = E[X²] − μ².

items.title: Linear momentum
items.subject: physics
items.body: p = mv. In an isolated system, total momentum is conserved. Force equals rate of change of momentum: F = dp/dt.

items.title: Work (mechanics)
items.subject: physics
items.body: W = ∫ F · dr. For a constant force along a straight displacement, W = F d cos θ. Work–energy: net work equals change in kinetic energy.

items.title: Electric potential
items.subject: physics
items.body: Electric potential V is potential energy per unit charge. For a point charge, V = kq/r (taking V(∞) = 0). Field E = −∇V.

items.title: Entropy (thermo)
items.subject: physics
items.body: For a reversible process dS = đQ_rev / T. Entropy of an isolated system never decreases (2nd law). Measures disorder / unavailable energy.

items.title: Mole
items.subject: chemistry
items.body: One mole contains N_A ≈ 6.022×10²³ elementary entities. Moles = mass / molar mass. Bridge between mass and particle count.

items.title: pH
items.subject: chemistry
items.body: pH = −log₁₀[H₃O⁺] (approx. in dilute aqueous solutions). Neutral water at 25 °C has pH 7; acids lower pH, bases raise it.

items.title: Equilibrium constant K
items.subject: chemistry
items.body: For aA + bB ⇌ cC + dD, K = ([C]^c [D]^d) / ([A]^a [B]^b) at equilibrium (activities; pure solids/liquids ≈ 1). K depends only on T.

items.title: Gene
items.subject: biology
items.body: A gene is a heritable unit of DNA that codes for a functional product (typically a protein or RNA). Alleles are alternative forms of a gene.

items.title: Enzyme
items.subject: biology
items.body: A biological catalyst (usually protein) that speeds a reaction by lowering activation energy without being consumed. Highly substrate-specific.

items.title: Homeostasis
items.subject: biology
items.body: Maintenance of stable internal conditions (temperature, pH, glucose, etc.) via feedback loops despite external changes.

items.title: Opportunity cost
items.subject: economics
items.body: The value of the next-best alternative forgone when a choice is made. True economic cost includes opportunity cost, not only cash outlay.

items.title: Price elasticity of demand
items.subject: economics
items.body: E_d = (%ΔQ_d) / (%ΔP). |E| > 1 elastic; |E| < 1 inelastic; |E| = 1 unit elastic. Determines how revenue responds to price changes.

items.title: GDP
items.subject: economics
items.body: Market value of all final goods and services produced within a country in a period. Expenditure: Y = C + I + G + (X − M).

items.title: Internal rate of return
items.subject: finance
items.body: Discount rate that makes NPV of a project’s cash flows equal zero. Accept if IRR exceeds the hurdle / cost of capital (with caveats).

items.title: Beta (CAPM)
items.subject: finance
items.body: Measures systematic risk: sensitivity of an asset’s excess return to market excess return. CAPM: $\mathrm{E}[R_{i}] = R_{f} + \beta_{i}(\mathrm{E}[R_{m}] - R_{f})$.

items.title: Macaulay duration
items.subject: finance
items.body: Weighted average time to receive a bond’s cash flows (weights = PV of each cash flow / price). Approximates interest-rate sensitivity.

items.title: FTC evaluation steps
items.subject: mathematics
listItems: Find antiderivative F with F′ = f
Watch sign: net area vs total area

items.title: Kinematics equation picker
items.subject: physics
listItems: Known a, t, v₀ → use v = v₀ + at
Known a, t, x₀ → use x = x₀ + v₀t + ½at²
Constant velocity → a = 0, x = x₀ + vt

items.title: Circuit analysis checklist
items.subject: physics
listItems: KCL: current into node = current out
KVL: sum of voltage drops around loop = 0
Series R: add; parallel R: reciprocal sum

items.title: Ideal gas toolkit
items.subject: chemistry
listItems: Boyle: P ∝ 1/V at fixed T, n
Charles: V ∝ T at fixed P, n
Dalton: P_total = Σ P_i for gases

items.title: Mitosis stages
items.subject: biology
listItems: Metaphase: chromosomes align at metaphase plate
Telophase: nuclei reform; cytokinesis divides cell

items.title: Central dogma flow
items.subject: biology
listItems: Reverse transcription in some viruses (RNA → DNA)

items.title: Market structures snapshot
items.subject: economics
listItems: Perfect competition: many firms, price takers
Monopolistic competition: many firms, differentiated products

items.title: Capital budgeting decision rules
items.subject: finance
listItems: IRR > hurdle rate → accept (with multiple-IRR caution)
Payback: prefer shorter; ignores TVM unless discounted
Mutually exclusive: choose higher NPV, not higher IRR alone

items.title: Bond price drivers
items.subject: finance
listItems: Credit risk / spreads also move price

items.title: Hypothesis test outline
items.subject: mathematics
listItems: Decide: reject H₀ or fail to reject

items.title: Don’t forget the chain rule
items.subject: mathematics
items.body: When differentiating composites (sin(3x), e^{x²}, ln(g(x))), multiply by the inner derivative. Missing g′ is the #1 error.

items.title: Absolute value in integrals
items.subject: mathematics
items.body: ∫|f| is total area. Split where f changes sign; don’t drop absolute value and integrate blindly.

items.title: Singular matrices
items.subject: mathematics
items.body: If det(A) = 0, A is not invertible. The homogeneous system Ax = 0 has nontrivial solutions; columns are linearly dependent.

items.title: g points down
items.subject: physics
items.body: Choose a consistent coordinate system. If +y is up, a_y = −g for free fall. Sign errors dominate kinematics mistakes.

items.title: Energy vs Newton
items.subject: physics
items.body: Conservative forces → potential energy and W = −ΔU. Nonconservative work (friction) removes mechanical energy: ΔK + ΔU = W_nc.

items.title: Right-hand rule
items.subject: physics
items.body: For F = q v × B and for magnetic field around a wire, use the right-hand rule carefully — direction mistakes flip the sign of F.

items.title: Limiting reagent first
items.subject: chemistry
items.body: Convert all reactants to moles of product using the balanced equation; the smallest product amount identifies the limiting reagent.

items.title: Strong vs weak acids
items.subject: chemistry
items.body: Strong acids fully dissociate (use [H⁺] ≈ C). Weak acids use K_a and ICE tables — do not treat them as fully dissociated.

items.title: Enthalpy sign convention
items.subject: chemistry
items.body: Exothermic: ΔH < 0 (system releases heat). Endothermic: ΔH > 0. Heat of reaction signs flip when the reaction is reversed.

items.title: Hardy–Weinberg assumptions
items.subject: biology
items.body: p² + 2pq + q² = 1 requires no selection, no migration, no mutation, random mating, and large population. Real pops often violate these.

items.title: ATP is not “energy stored forever”
items.subject: biology
items.body: ATP is a short-term energy currency. Cells continuously regenerate ATP from ADP + Pᵢ via respiration or photosynthesis pathways.

items.title: Ceteris paribus
items.subject: economics
items.body: Demand/supply shifts assume “all else equal.” If income and price change together, separate the movements carefully on the graph.

items.title: Real vs nominal
items.subject: economics
items.body: Nominal GDP uses current prices; real GDP holds prices fixed to isolate quantity changes. Growth rates usually quote real GDP.

items.title: Ignore sunk costs
items.subject: finance
items.body: Sunk costs already spent should not enter NPV. Only incremental future cash flows (including opportunity costs and cannibalization).

items.title: Match risk to discount rate
items.subject: finance
items.body: Discount project cash flows at a rate reflecting project risk. Using firm WACC for a riskier project overstates NPV.

items.title: Diversification limit
items.subject: finance
items.body: Diversification reduces idiosyncratic risk but not market (systematic) risk. β remains; CAPM prices only non-diversifiable risk.

items.title: Bisection root finder
items.subject: mathematics

items.title: Gradient descent step
items.subject: mathematics

items.title: Euler method (ODE)
items.subject: mathematics

items.title: Gaussian elimination sketch
items.subject: mathematics

items.title: ICE table workflow
items.subject: chemistry

items.title: Hardy–Weinberg genotype freqs
items.subject: biology
items.code:
# allele freqs p, q with p+q=1
AA = p**2
Aa = 2*p*q
aa = q**2
# check AA+Aa+aa ≈ 1

items.title: CAPM expected return (code)
items.subject: finance
items.description: Computational form of CAPM. Prefer catalog equation “CAPM expected return” (fin-capm) for the LaTeX card.

items.title: Black–Scholes d1, d2
items.subject: finance
items.code:
d1 = (ln(S/K) + (r + σ**2/2)*T) / (σ*sqrt(T))
d2 = d1 - σ*sqrt(T)
# call = S N(d1) - K e^{-rT} N(d2)

items.title: Standard gravity g
items.subject: physics
items.body: Often take g = 9.8 or 10 m/s² in rough problems.

items.title: Coulomb constant k_e
items.subject: physics
items.body: Also 1/(4πε₀).

items.title: Faraday's constant F
items.subject: chemistry
items.body: Charge of one mole of electrons ≈ N_A e.

items.title: Euler's number e
items.subject: mathematics
items.body: Base of natural logarithm; (e^x)′ = e^x.

items.title: Pi π
items.subject: mathematics
items.body: Circle circumference/diameter; appears in trig and complex analysis.

items.title: ln 2
items.subject: mathematics
items.body: Useful for half-life: t_{1/2} = ln 2 / λ.

items.title: 2×2 identity
items.subject: mathematics
items.description: I₂; AI = IA = A for compatible A.

items.title: Scaling matrix 2D
items.subject: mathematics
items.description: Scales x by s_x and y by s_y.

items.title: Horizontal shear
items.subject: mathematics
items.description: Shear parallel to x-axis by factor k.

items.title: 2×2 covariance template
items.subject: mathematics
items.description: Symmetric positive semidefinite for real data.

items.title: Projectile trajectory
items.subject: physics
items.description: Parabolic path under constant g (no drag).

items.title: Laffer curve (schematic)
items.subject: economics
items.description: Schematic only — peak location is empirical.

items.title: Problem-solving flow
items.subject: mathematics
items.mermaidSource:
flowchart TD
  A[Read problem] --> B[Identify givens]
  B --> C[Pick formula or method]
  C --> D[Compute carefully]
  D --> E[Check units]
  E --> F[State answer]

items.title: Differentiate workflow
items.subject: mathematics
items.mermaidSource:
flowchart TD
  A[Identify f] --> B{Which rule?}
  B -->|Chain| C[Inner then outer]
  B -->|Product| D["u v product"]
  B -->|Quotient| E["u over v"]
  C --> F[Simplify]
  D --> F
  E --> F

items.title: Integration technique picker
items.subject: mathematics
items.description: Choose u-sub, parts, or partial fractions.
items.mermaidSource:
flowchart TD
  A[Look at integrand] --> B{Composition form?}
  B -->|Yes| C[u-substitution]
  B -->|No| D{Unlike product?}
  D -->|Yes| E[Integration by parts]
  D -->|No| F{Rational?}
  F -->|Yes| G[Partial fractions]
  F -->|No| H[Table or rewrite]
  C --> I[Simplify]
  E --> I
  G --> I
  H --> I

items.title: Hypothesis test workflow
items.subject: mathematics
items.mermaidSource:
flowchart TD
  A["State H0 and Ha"] --> B["Choose alpha and test"]
  B --> C[Compute statistic]
  C --> D[p-value or critical]
  D --> E{Reject H0?}
  E -->|Yes| F[Significant]
  E -->|No| G[Fail to reject]
  F --> H[Context sentence]
  G --> H

items.title: Bayes update flow
items.subject: mathematics
items.mermaidSource:
flowchart LR
  A[Prior] --> B[Likelihood]
  B --> C[Evidence]
  C --> D[Posterior]
  D --> E[Decision]

items.title: NPV project screen
items.subject: finance
items.mermaidSource:
flowchart TD
  A[Estimate free cash flows] --> B[Choose discount rate]
  B --> C[Compute NPV IRR PI]
  C --> D{NPV positive?}
  D -->|Yes| E[Rank and fund]
  D -->|No| F[Reject or rework]
  E --> G[Sensitivity check]

items.title: Apply CAPM
items.subject: finance
items.description: Rf, beta, market premium to required return.
items.mermaidSource:
flowchart LR
  A[Get Rf] --> B[Estimate beta]
  B --> C[Estimate E Rm]
  C --> D[Compute E Ri]
  D --> E[Compare to forecast]

items.title: Supply/demand shock analysis
items.subject: economics
items.mermaidSource:
flowchart TD
  A[Identify shock] --> B{Demand or supply?}
  B -->|Demand| C[Shift D]
  B -->|Supply| D[Shift S]
  C --> E[New equilibrium]
  D --> E
  E --> F[Surplus or shortage path]

items.title: Lab method overview
items.subject: chemistry
items.mermaidSource:
flowchart TD
  A[Hypothesis] --> B[Design procedure]
  B --> C[Measure data]
  C --> D[Analyze error]
  D --> E[Conclusion]
  E --> F[Report]

items.title: 1D kinematics approach
items.subject: physics
items.mermaidSource:
flowchart TD
  A[List knowns and unknown] --> B{Constant a?}
  B -->|Yes| C[Pick kinematic equation]
  B -->|No| D[Use calculus or energy]
  C --> E[Solve algebraically]
  D --> E
  E --> F[Check units and sign]

items.title: Energy conservation flow
items.subject: physics
items.mermaidSource:
flowchart TD
  A[Define system] --> B[Initial energy]
  B --> C[Final energy]
  C --> D{Nonconservative work?}
  D -->|Yes| E["Delta E equals Wnc"]
  D -->|No| F["Ei equals Ef"]
  E --> G[Solve for unknown]
  F --> G

items.title: Limiting reagent workflow
items.subject: chemistry
items.mermaidSource:
flowchart TD
  A[Balance equation] --> B[Convert to moles]
  B --> C[Compare mole ratios]
  C --> D[Identify limiting reagent]
  D --> E[Compute product moles]
  E --> F[Mass or yield]

items.title: Monohybrid cross flow
items.subject: biology
items.mermaidSource:
flowchart TD
  A[Parental genotypes] --> B[Gametes]
  B --> C[Punnett square]
  C --> D[Offspring genotypes]
  D --> E[Phenotype ratios]

items.title: Exam problem checklist
items.subject: general
items.mermaidSource:
flowchart LR
  A[Read carefully] --> B[Identify topic]
  B --> C[Pick formula]
  C --> D[Plug numbers]
  D --> E[Units sanity]
  E --> F[Box answer]
